421: ## `adf-issue-stage` Implementation Detail
422: 
423: ### Stage Handlers
424: 
425: ```bash
426: #!/usr/bin/env bash
427: set -euo pipefail
428: 
429: task="${1:-}"
430: ADF_DOCS="/home/alex/projects/terraphim/terraphim-ai/.docs/adf"
431: GITEA_API="https://git.terraphim.cloud/api/v1"
432: 
433: # Extract issue number
434: if [[ "$task" =~ (^|[[:space:];])issue=([0-9]+) ]]; then
435:   issue="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
436: else
437:   # Auto-pick from gtr ready
438:   issue="$(gtr ready --owner terraphim --repo terraphim-ai | jq -r '.ready_issues[0].index // empty')"
439:   [[ "$issue" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || { printf 'No issue available\n' >&2; exit 2; }
440: fi
441: 
442: # Ensure artefact directory exists
443: issue_dir="${ADF_DOCS}/${issue}"
444: mkdir -p "$issue_dir"
445: 
446: # Read issue body from Gitea API
447: issue_body="$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: token ${GITEA_TOKEN}" \
448:   "${GITEA_API}/repos/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/${issue}" | jq -r '.body // ""')"
449: 
450: # Parse stage
451: case "$task" in
452:   stage=disciplined-research*) stage="research" ;;
453:   stage=disciplined-design*) stage="design" ;;
454:   stage=disciplined-implementation*) stage="implementation" ;;
455:   stage=structured-pr-review*|stage=pr-reviewer*) stage="review" ;;
456:   stage=disciplined-verification*) stage="verification" ;;
457:   stage=disciplined-validation*) stage="validation" ;;
458:   stage=corrections*) stage="corrections" ;;
459:   stage=security-sentinel*) stage="security" ;;
460:   stage=build-runner*) stage="build" ;;
461:   stage=meta-learning*) stage="meta-learning" ;;
462:   stage=implementation-swarm*) stage="implementation" ;;
463:   *) stage="unknown" ;;
464: esac
465: 
466: # Stage-specific logic
467: case "$stage" in
468:   research)
469:     artefact="${issue_dir}/research.md"
470:     # Check if artefact already exists
471:     if [[ -f "$artefact" ]]; then
472:       printf 'Research artefact already exists: %s\n' "$artefact" >&2
473:       exit 0
474:     fi
475:     # Write research artefact with frontmatter
476:     cat > "$artefact" <<ARTEFACT
477: ---
478: stage: research
479: issue: ${issue}
480: classification: pending
481: timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
482: agent: disciplined-research-agent
483: ---
484: 
485: ## Issue Summary
486: <!-- Agent fills this in -->
487: 
488: ## Current State
489: <!-- Agent analyses codebase -->
490: 
491: ## Classification Rationale
492: <!-- Agent classifies: valid/stale/duplicate/blocked/needs-rescope -->
493: 
494: ## Key Findings
495: <!-- Agent documents findings -->
496: 
497: ## Recommendations
498: <!-- Agent recommends next steps -->
499: ARTEFACT
500:     # Post structured comment
501:     gtr comment --owner terraphim --repo terraphim-ai --index "$issue" \
502:       --body "## Research started
503: 
504: Stage: disciplined-research
505: Issue: #${issue}
506: Artefact: \`${artefact}\`
507: 
508: Research artefact created. Agent should populate classification and findings."
509:     printf 'Research artefact created: %s\n' "$artefact"
510:     ;;
511: 
512:   design)
513:     # Requires research artefact to exist
514:     research_artefact="${issue_dir}/research.md"
515:     if [[ ! -f "$research_artefact" ]]; then
516:       printf 'Research artefact not found. Run research first.\n' >&2
517:       exit 1
518:     fi
519:     artefact="${issue_dir}/design.md"
520:     if [[ -f "$artefact" ]]; then
521:       printf 'Design artefact already exists: %s\n' "$artefact" >&2
522:       exit 0
523:     fi
524:     cat > "$artefact" <<ARTEFACT
525: ---
526: stage: design
527: issue: ${issue}
528: timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
529: agent: disciplined-design-agent
530: ---
531: 
532: ## Design Overview
533: <!-- Agent fills this in -->
534: 
535: ## File Changes
536: <!-- Agent specifies file changes -->
537: 
538: ## Implementation Steps
539: <!-- Agent provides numbered steps -->
540: 
541: ## Test Strategy
542: <!-- Agent defines verification approach -->
543: ARTEFACT
544:     gtr comment --owner terraphim --repo terraphim-ai --index "$issue" \
545:       --body "## Design started
546: 
547: Stage: disciplined-design
548: Issue: #${issue}
549: Artefact: \`${artefact}\`
550: 
551: Design artefact created. Agent should populate implementation plan."
552:     printf 'Design artefact created: %s\n' "$artefact"
553:     ;;
554: 
555:   review)
556:     artefact="${issue_dir}/review-findings.json"
557:     cat > "$artefact" <<ARTEFACT
558: {
559:   "stage": "review",
560:   "issue": ${issue},
561:   "timestamp": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
562:   "agent": "structured-pr-review",
563:   "findings": [],
564:   "outstanding": false,
565:   "summary": "pending"
566: }
567: ARTEFACT
568:     printf 'Review artefact created: %s\n' "$artefact"
569:     ;;
570: 
571:   corrections)
572:     review_artefact="${issue_dir}/review-findings.json"
573:     if [[ ! -f "$review_artefact" ]]; then
574:       printf 'No review findings. Nothing to correct.\n'
575:       exit 0
576:     fi
577:     outstanding="$(jq -r '.outstanding // false' "$review_artefact")"
578:     p0_count="$(jq '[.findings[] | select(.severity == "P0")] | length' "$review_artefact")"
579:     p1_count="$(jq '[.findings[] | select(.severity == "P1")] | length' "$review_artefact")"
580:     if [[ "$outstanding" == "true" ]] && [[ $((p0_count + p1_count)) -gt 0 ]]; then
581:       adf_ctl="/home/alex/projects/terraphim/terraphim-ai/target/debug/adf-ctl"
582:       "$adf_ctl" --local trigger disciplined-research-agent --direct --context "issue=${issue} reason=corrections-p0-p1"
583:       "$adf_ctl" --local trigger disciplined-design-agent --direct --context "issue=${issue} reason=corrections-p0-p1"
584:       printf 'Corrections: re-triggered research + design for P0/P1 findings\n'
585:     else
586:       printf 'Corrections: no outstanding P0/P1 findings\n'
587:     fi
588:     ;;
589: 
590:   *)
591:     # Generic stages: build, security, meta-learning, verification, validation
592:     artefact="${issue_dir}/${stage}.md"
593:     cat > "$artefact" <<ARTEFACT
594: ---
595: stage: ${stage}
596: issue: ${issue}
597: timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
598: agent: ${stage}
599: ---
600: 
601: ## ${stage} output
602: <!-- Agent populates stage-specific output -->
603: ARTEFACT
604:     printf '%s artefact created: %s\n' "$stage" "$artefact"
605:     ;;
606: esac
607: ```
608: 
609: ### Corrections Contract
610: 
611: The corrections stage is driven by `review-findings.json`, not manual flags:
612: 
613: 1. Read `review-findings.json`
614: 2. Check `outstanding` field
615: 3. Count P0 and P1 findings
616: 4. If `outstanding == true` AND P0+P1 > 0: re-trigger research + design
617: 5. Otherwise: exit 0 with log message
618: 6. Never post a comment unless re-triggering (and even then, only the re-triggered stages comment)
619:

3. New `adf-flow-runner` script that invokes the orchestrator's flow engine locally
4. `boosting.toml` configuration for per-phase model selection

1: # k=3 research+design flow with disciplined skills.
2: # Run with: adf-ctl flow zdp-research --context "issue=1882"
3: #
4: # Steps:
5: #   1. matrix-research: 3 parallel proposals using disciplined-research skill (KLS evaluation)
6: #   2. gate-check: require at least 2 research proposals to succeed
7: #   3. matrix-design: 3 parallel design proposals using disciplined-design skill
8: #   4. gate-check: require at least 2 design proposals to succeed
9: #   5. synthesize: judge-evaluates designs -> design-synthesis.md
10: #   6. checkpoint: pause for human review
11: #
12: # Models:
13: #   - Slot 1: opus via Claude CLI (primary), kimi fallback on rate limit
14: #   - Slot 2: kimi-for-coding/k2p6 via opencode
15: #   - Slot 3: openai/gpt-5.5 via opencode
16: 
17: name = "zdp-research"
18: project = "terraphim-ai"
19: repo_path = "/home/alex/projects/terraphim/terraphim-ai"
20: timeout_secs = 3600
21: 
22: # ============================================================
23: # Step 1: Research - disciplined-research skill (KLS evaluation)
24: # ============================================================
25: [[steps]]
26: name = "matrix-research"
27: kind = "agent"
28: cli_tool = "opencode"
29: task = "You are running the disciplined-research skill for issue #{{issue}}.\n\nYour task:\n1. Load and apply the disciplined-research skill: skill://disciplined-research\n2. Read the issue at https://git.terraphim.cloud/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/{{issue}}\n3. Apply the KLS 6-dimension framework to evaluate the research quality:\n   - Explicitity (are requirements unambiguous?)\n   - External Consistency (does it fit existing architecture?)\n   - Internal Consistency (are goals coherent?)\n   - Stakeholder Commitment (are stakeholders aligned?)\n   - Information Quality (is evidence solid?)\n   - Overall Coherence (does it hang together?)\n4. Write your research proposal to .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-proposal-{{matrix.slot}}.md\n5. Classify the issue as: valid, stale, duplicate, blocked, or needs-rescope\n\nResearch proposal format (YAML frontmatter + markdown):\n---\nstage: research-proposal\nissue: {{issue}}\nslot: {{matrix.slot}}\nmodel: {{matrix.model}}\nprovider: {{matrix.provider}}\ntimestamp: FILL_IN\nclassification: FILL_IN\nkls_scores:\n  explicitity: FILL_IN\n  external_consistency: FILL_IN\n  internal_consistency: FILL_IN\n  stakeholder_commitment: FILL_IN\n  information_quality: FILL_IN\n  overall_coherence: FILL_IN\n---\n\n## Issue Summary\n[1 paragraph: what the issue asks for]\n\n## KLS Evaluation\n[Rate each dimension 1-5 with justification]\n\n## Classification\n[valid|stale|duplicate|blocked|needs-rescope with rationale]\n\n## Key Findings\n- [Finding 1]\n- [Finding 2]\n\n## Recommendations\n[What should happen next]\n"
30: model = "{{matrix.model}}"
31: provider = "{{matrix.provider}}"
32: timeout_secs = 600
33: on_fail = "continue"
34: 
35: [steps.matrix]
36: max_parallel = 3
37: fail_strategy = "continue"
38: 
39: [[steps.matrix.params]]
40: slot = "1"
41: model = "opus"
42: provider = "claude"
43: cli_tool = "claude"
44: 
45: [[steps.matrix.params]]
46: slot = "2"
47: model = "kimi-for-coding/k2p6"
48: provider = "kimi"
49: cli_tool = "opencode"
50: 
51: [[steps.matrix.params]]
52: slot = "3"
53: model = "openai/gpt-5.5"
54: provider = "openai"
55: cli_tool = "opencode"
56: 
57: # ============================================================
58: # Step 2: Gate - require 2+ research proposals
59: # ============================================================
60: [[steps]]
61: name = "gate-research"
62: kind = "gate"
63: condition = "{{steps.matrix-research.success_count}} >= 2"
64: on_fail = "abort"
65: 
66: # ============================================================
67: # Step 3: Design - disciplined-design skill
68: # ============================================================
69: [[steps]]
70: name = "matrix-design"
71: kind = "agent"
72: cli_tool = "opencode"
73: task = "You are running the disciplined-design skill for issue #{{issue}}.\n\nYour task:\n1. Load and apply the disciplined-design skill: skill://disciplined-design\n2. Read the research synthesis at .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-synthesis.md\n3. Read the research proposals at:\n   - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-proposal-1.md\n   - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-proposal-2.md\n   - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-proposal-3.md\n4. Create a design proposal using disciplined-design methodology\n5. Write your design to .docs/adf/{{issue}}/design-proposal-{{matrix.slot}}.md\n\nDesign proposal format (YAML frontmatter + markdown):\n---\nstage: design-proposal\nissue: {{issue}}\nslot: {{matrix.slot}}\nmodel: {{matrix.model}}\nprovider: {{matrix.provider}}\ntimestamp: FILL_IN\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n[What this design solves]\n\n## Architecture\n[Proposed architecture with trade-offs]\n\n## Implementation Plan\n[Step-by-step implementation]\n\n## File Changes\n[List of files to create/modify]\n\n## Risks and Mitigations\n[Identified risks and how to mitigate]\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n[How to verify the implementation]\n"
74: model = "{{matrix.model}}"
75: provider = "{{matrix.provider}}"
76: timeout_secs = 900
77: on_fail = "continue"
78: 
79: # ============================================================
80: # Step 4: Gate - require 2+ design proposals
81: # ============================================================
82: [[steps]]
83: name = "gate-design"
84: kind = "gate"
85: condition = "{{steps.matrix-design.success_count}} >= 2"
86: on_fail = "abort"
87: 
88: # ============================================================
89: # Step 5: Judge - evaluate designs (not research)
90: # ============================================================
91: [[steps]]
92: name = "synthesize"
93: kind = "agent"
94: cli_tool = "opencode"
95: model = "kimi-for-coding/k2p6"
96: provider = "kimi"
97: task = "You are the judge for a k=3 design panel on issue #{{issue}}.\n\nRead the 3 design proposals at:\n  - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/design-proposal-1.md\n  - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/design-proposal-2.md\n  - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/design-proposal-3.md\n\nRead the research synthesis for context:\n  - .docs/adf/{{issue}}/research-synthesis.md\n\nYour task: judge-evaluate the 3 design proposals and write a synthesis to:\n.docs/adf/{{issue}}/design-synthesis.md\n\nEvaluate each design on:\n- Completeness (covers all acceptance criteria)\n- Feasibility (can be implemented in scope)\n- Risk (identifies and mitigates risks)\n- Consistency (consistent with existing architecture)\n- KLS Quality (use KLS 6-dimension framework)\n\nDesign synthesis format:\n---\nstage: design-synthesis\nissue: {{issue}}\ntimestamp: FILL_IN\nchosen_design: [slot number]\n---\n\n## Best Design\n[Which design is best and why]\n\n## Design Evaluation\n[Comparison of all 3 designs against criteria]\n\n## Recommended Implementation\n[Hybrid approach if needed, or single best design]\n\n## Open Questions\n[Questions to resolve before implementation]\n"
98: timeout_secs = 600
99: 
100: # ============================================================
101: # Step 6: Human review checkpoint
102: # ============================================================
103: [[steps]]
104: name = "human-review"
105: kind = "checkpoint"

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# k=3 research+design flow with disciplined skills.

#   4. gate-check: require at least 2 design proposals to succeed
#   5. synthesize: judge-evaluates designs -> design-synthesis.md
#   6. checkpoint: pause for human review
#
# Models:
#   - Slot 1: opus via Claude CLI (primary), kimi fallback on rate limit
#   - Slot 2: kimi-for-coding/k2p6 via opencode
#   - Slot 3: openai/gpt-5.5 via opencode

project = "terraphim-ai"

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # adf-issue-stage: Real stage logic for ADF.
3: # Each stage produces a structured artefact in .docs/adf/<issue>/
4: # No template comments -- only meaningful output is posted to Gitea.
5: set -euo pipefail
6: 
7: ADF_DOCS="${ADF_DOCS:-/home/alex/projects/terraphim/terraphim-ai/.docs/adf}"
8: GITEA_API="${GITEA_API:-https://git.terraphim.cloud/api/v1}"
9: GITEA_TOKEN="${GITEA_TOKEN:-}"
10: ADF_CTL="${ADF_CTL:-/home/alex/projects/terraphim/terraphim-ai/target/debug/adf-ctl}"
11: 
12: task="${1:-}"
13: issue=""
14: 
15: # --- Context parsing ---
16: 
17: extract_issue_from_task() {
18:     local t="$1"
19:     if [[ "$t" =~ (^|[[:space:];])issue=([0-9]+) ]]; then
20:         printf '%s' "${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
21:         return 0
22:     fi
23:     return 1
24: }
25: 
26: extract_artefact_from_task() {
27:     local t="$1"
28:     if [[ "$t" =~ (^|[[:space:];])artefact=([^[:space:];]+) ]]; then
29:         printf '%s' "${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
30:         return 0
31:     fi
32:     return 1
33: }
34: 
35: has_task_flag() {
36:     local flag="$1"
37:     local t="$task"
38:     [[ "$t" =~ (^|[[:space:];])${flag}(=true)?($|[[:space:];]) ]]
39: }
40: 
41: pick_ready_issue() {
42:     if ! command -v gtr >/dev/null 2>&1; then
43:         printf 'ERROR: gtr not available for auto-pick\n' >&2
44:         return 1
45:     fi
46:     local ready_json
47:     ready_json="$(gtr ready --owner terraphim --repo terraphim-ai 2>/dev/null)" || return 1
48:     if command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
49:         jq -r '.ready_issues[0].index // empty' <<< "$ready_json" 2>/dev/null || return 1
50:     else
51:         printf '%s' "$ready_json" | sed -n 's/.*"ready_issues":\[{"id":[0-9][0-9]*,"index":\([0-9][0-9]*\).*/\1/p' | head -1
52:     fi
53: }
54: 
55: # --- Issue context ---
56: 
57: fetch_issue_body() {
58:     local iss="$1"
59:     if [[ -n "$GITEA_TOKEN" ]]; then
60:         curl -sf -H "Authorization: token ${GITEA_TOKEN}" \
61:             "${GITEA_API}/repos/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/${iss}" 2>/dev/null | \
62:             jq -r '.body // empty'
63:     else
64:         curl -sf \
65:             "${GITEA_API}/repos/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/${iss}" 2>/dev/null | \
66:             jq -r '.body // empty'
67:     fi
68: }
69: 
70: fetch_issue_title() {
71:     local iss="$1"
72:     if [[ -n "$GITEA_TOKEN" ]]; then
73:         curl -sf -H "Authorization: token ${GITEA_TOKEN}" \
74:             "${GITEA_API}/repos/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/${iss}" 2>/dev/null | \
75:             jq -r '.title // empty'
76:     else
77:         curl -sf \
78:             "${GITEA_API}/repos/terraphim/terraphim-ai/issues/${iss}" 2>/dev/null | \
79:             jq -r '.title // empty'
80:     fi
81: }
82: 
83: # --- Artefact helpers ---
84: 
85: ensure_issue_dir() {
86:     local iss="$1"
87:     mkdir -p "${ADF_DOCS}/${iss}"
88: }
89: 
90: write_frontmatter_artefact() {
91:     local path="$1"
92:     local stage="$2"
93:     local iss="$3"
94:     local classification="${4:-pending}"
95:     local agent="${5:-adf-issue-stage}"
96: 
97:     cat > "$path" <<EOF
98: ---
99: stage: ${stage}
100: issue: ${iss}
101: classification: ${classification}
102: timestamp: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
103: agent: ${agent}
104: ---
105: 
106: ## Summary
107: <!-- Fill in -->
108: 
109: ## Details
110: <!-- Fill in -->
111: 
112: ## Classification Rationale
113: <!-- Fill in (for research stage) -->
114: EOF
115: }
116: 
117: post_structured_comment() {
118:     local iss="$1"
119:     local stage="$2"
120:     local artefact_path="$3"
121:     local summary="${4:-}"
122: 
123:     if [[ -z "$summary" ]]; then
124:         summary="Stage '${stage}' completed for issue #${iss}"
125:     fi
126: 
127:     local body="## ADF ${stage^} Progress
128: 
129: **Issue:** #${iss}
130: **Stage:** ${stage}
131: **Artefact:** \`${artefact_path}\`
132: 
133: ${summary}"
134: 
135:     if command -v gtr >/dev/null 2>&1; then
136:         gtr comment --owner terraphim --repo terraphim-ai --index "$iss" --body "$body" 2>/dev/null || {
137:             printf 'WARNING: gtr comment failed for issue #%s\n' "$iss" >&2
138:         }
139:     fi
140: }
141: 
142: # --- Stage handlers ---
143: 
144: handle_research() {
145:     local iss="$1"
146:     local artefact="${ADF_DOCS}/${iss}/research.md"
147: 
148:     ensure_issue_dir "$iss"
149: 
150:     if [[ -f "$artefact" ]]; then
151:         printf 'Research artefact already exists: %s\n' "$artefact"
152:         return 0
153:     fi
154: 
155:     local title
156:     title="$(fetch_issue_title "$iss")"
157:     local body
158:     body="$(fetch_issue_body "$iss")"
159: 
160:     write_frontmatter_artefact "$artefact" "research" "$iss" "pending"
161: 
162:     cat >> "$artefact" <<EOF
163: 
164: ## Issue Title
165: ${title}
166: 
167: ## Issue Body
168: \`\`\`
169: ${body}

485: parse_stage() {
486:     local t="$1"
487:     case "$t" in
488:         stage=disciplined-research*)  printf 'research' ;;
489:         stage=disciplined-design*)      printf 'design' ;;
490:         stage=disciplined-implementation*) printf 'implementation' ;;
491:         stage=structured-pr-review*|stage=pr-reviewer*) printf 'review' ;;
492:         stage=disciplined-verification*) printf 'verification' ;;
493:         stage=disciplined-validation*)  printf 'validation' ;;
494:         stage=corrections*)             printf 'corrections' ;;
495:         stage=security-sentinel*)       printf 'security' ;;
496:         stage=build-runner*)           printf 'build' ;;
497:         stage=meta-learning*)           printf 'meta-learning' ;;
498:         stage=implementation-swarm*)    printf 'implementation' ;;
499:         *)                             printf 'unknown' ;;
500:     esac
501: }
502: 
503: main() {
504:     if [[ -z "$task" ]]; then
505:         printf 'ERROR: no task string provided\n' >&2
506:         printf 'Usage: %s \"stage=<stage>; issue=<number>\"\n' "$0" >&2
507:         exit 2
508:     fi
509: 
510:     if ! issue="$(extract_issue_from_task "$task")"; then
511:         printf 'No issue in task string, auto-picking...\n' >&2
512:         if ! issue="$(pick_ready_issue)"; then
513:             printf 'ERROR: no issue available and gtr not available\n' >&2
514:             exit 2
515:         fi
516:         task="${task}; issue=${issue} auto-picked-by=adf-issue-stage"
517:         printf 'Auto-picked issue: %s\n' "$issue"
518:     fi
519: 
520:     local stage
521:     stage="$(parse_stage "$task")"
522:     printf 'Stage: %s, Issue: %s\n' "$stage" "$issue"
523: 
524:     case "$stage" in
525:         research)    handle_research "$issue" ;;
526:         design)      handle_design "$issue" ;;
527:         implementation) handle_implementation "$issue" ;;
528:         review)     handle_review "$issue" ;;
529:         corrections) handle_corrections "$issue" ;;
530:         verification) handle_verification "$issue" ;;
531:         validation)  handle_validation "$issue" ;;
532:         security)    handle_security "$issue" ;;
533:         build)       handle_build "$issue" ;;
534:         meta-learning) handle_meta_learning "$issue" ;;
535:         *)           handle_unknown "$issue" "$stage" ;;
536:     esac
537: }
538: 
539: main "$@"

18: /// Shell metacharacters that must not appear in matrix param values.
19: /// Param values are substituted verbatim into `bash -lc` command strings;
20: /// any of these characters can introduce injection when the command is executed.
21: const SHELL_METACHARACTERS: &[char] = &[';', '|', '&', '`', '$', '>', '<', '!', '\n', '(', ')'];
22: 
23: /// Validate a single matrix param value against shell injection.
24: /// Returns `Err` if the value contains any shell metacharacter.
25: fn validate_matrix_param(
26:     flow_name: &str,
27:     step_name: &str,
28:     key: &str,
29:     value: &str,
30: ) -> Result<(), OrchestratorError> {
31:     for &ch in SHELL_METACHARACTERS {
32:         if value.contains(ch) {
33:             return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
34:                 flow_name: flow_name.to_string(),
35:                 reason: format!(
36:                     "matrix step '{}': param '{}' contains shell metacharacter '{}'; \
37:                      only trusted, shell-safe values are permitted",
38:                     step_name, key, ch
39:                 ),
40:             });
41:         }
42:     }
43:     Ok(())
44: }

46: /// Replace `{{matrix.<key>}}` placeholders in `template` with values from `row`.
47: /// Unresolved placeholders are left as-is (will be cleared by resolve_templates later).
48: fn resolve_matrix_vars(template: &str, row: &MatrixParams) -> String {
49:     let mut result = template.to_string();
50:     for (key, value) in row {
51:         let placeholder = format!("{{{{matrix.{}}}}}", key);
52:         result = result.replace(&placeholder, value);
53:     }
54:     result
55: }

57: /// Per-project runtime metadata used to build a [`SpawnContext`] for flow
58: /// steps. Populated from `OrchestratorConfig.projects` when FlowExecutor is
59: /// constructed.
60: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
61: pub struct ProjectRuntime {
62:     pub working_dir: PathBuf,
63:     pub gitea_owner: Option<String>,
64:     pub gitea_repo: Option<String>,
65: }
66: 
67: pub struct FlowExecutor {
68:     pub working_dir: PathBuf,
69:     pub spawner: AgentSpawner,
70:     pub flow_state_dir: PathBuf,
71:     /// Per-project runtime metadata, keyed by project id. Missing entries
72:     /// mean "use the FlowExecutor's top-level working_dir" (legacy mode).
73:     pub projects: HashMap<String, ProjectRuntime>,
74: }

76: impl FlowExecutor {
77:     pub fn new(working_dir: PathBuf, flow_state_dir: PathBuf) -> Self {
78:         Self {
79:             working_dir: working_dir.clone(),
80:             spawner: AgentSpawner::new().with_working_dir(&working_dir),
81:             flow_state_dir,
82:             projects: HashMap::new(),
83:         }
84:     }
85: 
86:     /// Builder: attach per-project runtime metadata.
87:     pub fn with_projects(mut self, projects: HashMap<String, ProjectRuntime>) -> Self {
88:         self.projects = projects;
89:         self
90:     }

92:     /// Build a [`SpawnContext`] for the given flow's project. If the project
93:     /// id is unknown or legacy, returns [`SpawnContext::global()`].
94:     fn spawn_context_for_flow(&self, flow: &FlowDefinition) -> SpawnContext {
95:         let Some(runtime) = self.projects.get(&flow.project) else {
96:             return SpawnContext::global();
97:         };
98:         let working_dir_str = runtime.working_dir.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
99:         let mut ctx = SpawnContext::with_working_dir(runtime.working_dir.clone())
100:             .with_env("ADF_PROJECT_ID", flow.project.clone())
101:             .with_env("ADF_WORKING_DIR", working_dir_str);
102:         if let Some(owner) = runtime.gitea_owner.as_deref() {
103:             ctx = ctx.with_env("GITEA_OWNER", owner.to_string());
104:         }
105:         if let Some(repo) = runtime.gitea_repo.as_deref() {
106:             ctx = ctx.with_env("GITEA_REPO", repo.to_string());
107:         }
108:         ctx
109:     }

111:     /// Execute an action step (shell command).
112:     /// Runs the command via `bash -lc` to get login shell environment.
113:     /// Captures stdout, stderr, and exit code into a StepEnvelope.
114:     pub async fn execute_action(
115:         &self,
116:         step: &FlowStepDef,
117:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
118:         state: &FlowRunState,
119:     ) -> Result<StepEnvelope, OrchestratorError> {
120:         let command = step
121:             .command
122:             .as_ref()
123:             .ok_or_else(|| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
124:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
125:                 reason: format!("action step '{}' missing 'command' field", step.name),
126:             })?;
127: 
128:         let resolved_command = self.resolve_templates(command, flow, state);
129:         let started_at = Utc::now();
130: 
131:         let result = timeout(
132:             Duration::from_secs(step.timeout_secs),
133:             Command::new("bash")
134:                 .arg("-lc")
135:                 .arg(&resolved_command)
136:                 .current_dir(&self.working_dir)
137:                 .output(),
138:         )
139:         .await;
140: 
141:         match result {
142:             Ok(Ok(output)) => {
143:                 let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string();
144:                 let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string();
145: 
146:                 // Parse token usage from combined output
147:                 let combined_output = format!("{} {}", stdout, stderr);
148:                 let token_usage = crate::flow::token_parser::parse_token_usage(&combined_output);
149: 
150:                 let mut envelope = StepEnvelope {
151:                     step_name: step.name.clone(),
152:                     started_at,
153:                     finished_at: Utc::now(),
154:                     exit_code: output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
155:                     stdout,
156:                     stderr,
157:                     cost_usd: token_usage.cost_usd,
158:                     session_id: None,
159:                     input_tokens: token_usage.input_tokens,
160:                     output_tokens: token_usage.output_tokens,
161:                     stdout_file: None,
162:                 };
163:                 envelope.truncate_output();
164:                 Ok(envelope)
165:             }
166:             Ok(Err(e)) => Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
167:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
168:                 reason: format!("action step '{}' spawn failed: {}", step.name, e),
169:             }),
170:             Err(_) => Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
171:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
172:                 reason: format!(
173:                     "action step '{}' timed out after {}s",
174:                     step.name, step.timeout_secs
175:                 ),
176:             }),
177:         }
178:     }
179: 
180:     /// Evaluate a gate condition against completed step outputs.
181:     /// Supports simple expressions: `steps.<name>.exit_code == 0`,
182:     /// `steps.<name>.exit_code != 0`, and numeric comparisons such as
183:     /// `steps.<name>.success_count >= 2`.
184:     pub fn evaluate_gate(
185:         &self,
186:         step: &FlowStepDef,
187:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
188:         state: &FlowRunState,
189:     ) -> Result<bool, OrchestratorError> {
190:         let condition = step
191:             .condition
192:             .as_ref()
193:             .ok_or_else(|| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
194:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
195:                 reason: format!("gate step '{}' missing 'condition' field", step.name),
196:             })?;
197: 
198:         let resolved = self.resolve_templates(condition, flow, state);
199: 
200:         for op in [" >= ", " <= ", " > ", " < ", " == ", " != "] {
201:             if let Some((lhs, rhs)) = resolved.split_once(op) {
202:                 let lhs = lhs.trim();
203:                 let rhs = rhs.trim();
204:                 return match op.trim() {
205:                     "==" => Ok(lhs == rhs),
206:                     "!=" => Ok(lhs != rhs),
207:                     ">=" | "<=" | ">" | "<" => {
208:                         let lhs_num = lhs.parse::<i64>().map_err(|_| {
209:                             OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
210:                                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
211:                                 reason: format!(
212:                                     "gate step '{}': left side '{}' is not numeric in condition: {}",
213:                                     step.name, lhs, resolved
214:                                 ),
215:                             }
216:                         })?;
217:                         let rhs_num = rhs.parse::<i64>().map_err(|_| {
218:                             OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
219:                                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
220:                                 reason: format!(
221:                                     "gate step '{}': right side '{}' is not numeric in condition: {}",
222:                                     step.name, rhs, resolved
223:                                 ),
224:                             }
225:                         })?;
226:                         match op.trim() {
227:                             ">=" => Ok(lhs_num >= rhs_num),
228:                             "<=" => Ok(lhs_num <= rhs_num),
229:                             ">" => Ok(lhs_num > rhs_num),
230:                             "<" => Ok(lhs_num < rhs_num),
231:                             _ => unreachable!(),
232:                         }
233:                     }
234:                     _ => unreachable!(),
235:                 };
236:             }
237:         }
238: 
239:         Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
240:             flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
241:             reason: format!(
242:                 "gate step '{}': unsupported condition expression: {}",
243:                 step.name, resolved
244:             ),
245:         })
246:     }

248:     /// Execute an agent step by spawning a CLI tool.
249:     /// Creates an AgentDefinition from the FlowStepDef and uses the spawner.
250:     pub async fn execute_agent(
251:         &self,
252:         step: &FlowStepDef,
253:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
254:         state: &FlowRunState,
255:     ) -> Result<StepEnvelope, OrchestratorError> {
256:         let cli_tool = step
257:             .cli_tool
258:             .as_ref()
259:             .ok_or_else(|| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
260:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
261:                 reason: format!("agent step '{}' missing 'cli_tool' field", step.name),
262:             })?;
263: 
264:         // Get task from task_file or task field
265:         let task = if let Some(ref task_file) = step.task_file {
266:             let path = if std::path::Path::new(task_file).is_absolute() {
267:                 std::path::PathBuf::from(task_file)
268:             } else {
269:                 self.working_dir.join(task_file)
270:             };
271:             std::fs::read_to_string(&path).map_err(|e| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
272:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
273:                 reason: format!(
274:                     "agent step '{}' failed to read task_file '{}': {}",
275:                     step.name,
276:                     path.display(),
277:                     e
278:                 ),
279:             })?
280:         } else if let Some(ref task) = step.task {
281:             self.resolve_templates(task, flow, state)
282:         } else {
283:             return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
284:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
285:                 reason: format!(
286:                     "agent step '{}' missing both 'task' and 'task_file' fields",
287:                     step.name
288:                 ),
289:             });
290:         };
291: 
292:         let started_at = Utc::now();
293: 
294:         // Create an AgentDefinition from the step
295:         let agent_def = AgentDefinition {
296:             name: format!("flow-{}-{}", flow.name, step.name),
297:             layer: AgentLayer::Core,
298:             cli_tool: cli_tool.clone(),
299:             task: task.clone(),
300:             model: step.model.clone(),
301:             schedule: None,
302:             capabilities: vec![],
303:             max_memory_bytes: None,
304:             budget_monthly_cents: None,
305:             provider: step.provider.clone(),
306:             persona: step.persona.clone(),
307:             terraphim_role: None,
308:             skill_chain: vec![],
309:             sfia_skills: vec![],
310:             fallback_provider: step.fallback_provider.clone(),
311:             fallback_model: step.fallback_model.clone(),
312:             grace_period_secs: None,
313:             max_cpu_seconds: None,
314:             pre_check: None,
315:             gitea_issue: None,
316:             event_only: false,
317:             project: None,
318:             evolution_enabled: false,
319:             rlm_enabled: None,
320:             bypass_kg_routing: false,
321:             enabled: true,
322:         };
323: 
324:         // Build provider for spawner
325:         let provider = Provider {
326:             id: agent_def.name.clone(),
327:             name: agent_def.name.clone(),
328:             provider_type: terraphim_types::capability::ProviderType::Agent {
329:                 agent_id: agent_def.name.clone(),
330:                 cli_command: agent_def.cli_tool.clone(),
331:                 working_dir: self.working_dir.clone(),
332:             },
333:             capabilities: vec![],
334:             cost_level: terraphim_types::capability::CostLevel::Cheap,
335:             latency: terraphim_types::capability::Latency::Medium,
336:             keywords: vec![],
337:         };
338: 
339:         // Build spawn request
340:         let mut request = SpawnRequest::new(provider, &agent_def.task);
341: 
342:         if let Some(ref model) = agent_def.model {
343:             request = request.with_primary_model(model);
344:         }
345: 
346:         // Set fallback provider and model for rate limit scenarios
347:         // For opencode-based fallbacks, use the same CLI tool; for claude, use claude CLI
348:         if let (Some(ref fb_provider), Some(ref fb_model)) =
349:             (&agent_def.fallback_provider, &agent_def.fallback_model)
350:         {
351:             let fb_cli = if fb_provider == "claude" || fb_provider == "claude-code" {
352:                 "claude".to_string()
353:             } else {
354:                 // For kimi, openai, etc. use opencode
355:                 cli_tool.clone()
356:             };
357:             let fallback_provider = Provider {
358:                 id: format!("{}-fallback", agent_def.name),
359:                 name: fb_provider.clone(),
360:                 provider_type: terraphim_types::capability::ProviderType::Agent {
361:                     agent_id: format!("{}-fallback", agent_def.name),
362:                     cli_command: fb_cli,
363:                     working_dir: self.working_dir.clone(),
364:                 },
365:                 capabilities: vec![],
366:                 cost_level: terraphim_types::capability::CostLevel::Cheap,
367:                 latency: terraphim_types::capability::Latency::Medium,
368:                 keywords: vec![],
369:             };
370:             request = request.with_fallback_provider(fallback_provider);
371:             request = request.with_fallback_model(fb_model);
372:         }
373: 
374:         // Spawn the agent using the flow's project context
375:         let spawn_ctx = self.spawn_context_for_flow(flow);
376:         let mut handle = self
377:             .spawner
378:             .spawn_with_fallback(&request, spawn_ctx)
379:             .await
380:             .map_err(|e| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
381:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
382:                 reason: format!("agent step '{}' spawn failed: {}", step.name, e),
383:             })?;
384: 
385:         // Subscribe to output events BEFORE waiting for completion
386:         let mut output_rx = handle.subscribe_output();
387: 
388:         // Wait for process to exit naturally (with timeout)
389:         let wait_result = timeout(Duration::from_secs(step.timeout_secs), handle.wait()).await;
390: 
391:         let finished_at = Utc::now();
392: 
393:         // Try to get exit status
394:         let exit_code = match wait_result {
395:             Ok(Ok(status)) => status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
396:             Ok(Err(_)) => -1,
397:             Err(_) => {
398:                 // Timeout - kill the process
399:                 let _ = handle.shutdown(Duration::from_secs(5)).await;
400:                 return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
401:                     flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
402:                     reason: format!(
403:                         "agent step '{}' timed out after {}s",
404:                         step.name, step.timeout_secs
405:                     ),
406:                 });
407:             }
408:         };
409: 
410:         // Drain output events to capture stdout and stderr
411:         let mut stdout_lines = Vec::new();
412:         let mut stderr_lines = Vec::new();
413:         while let Ok(event) = output_rx.try_recv() {
414:             match event {
415:                 OutputEvent::Stdout { line, .. } => stdout_lines.push(line),
416:                 OutputEvent::Stderr { line, .. } => stderr_lines.push(line),
417:                 _ => {}
418:             }
419:         }
420: 
421:         // Parse token usage from combined output
422:         let stdout = stdout_lines.join("\n");
423:         let stderr = stderr_lines.join("\n");
424:         let combined_output = format!("{} {}", stdout, stderr);
425:         let token_usage = crate::flow::token_parser::parse_token_usage(&combined_output);
426: 
427:         // Build envelope with captured output
428:         let mut envelope = StepEnvelope {
429:             step_name: step.name.clone(),
430:             started_at,
431:             finished_at,
432:             exit_code,
433:             stdout,
434:             stderr,
435:             cost_usd: token_usage.cost_usd,
436:             session_id: None,
437:             input_tokens: token_usage.input_tokens,
438:             output_tokens: token_usage.output_tokens,
439:             stdout_file: None,
440:         };
441: 
442:         // Truncate stdout and stderr if they exceed limits
443:         envelope.truncate_output();
444: 
445:         Ok(envelope)
446:     }

448:     /// Run a flow from start (or resume from checkpoint state).
449:     /// This method MUST be tokio::spawn'd by the caller (never awaited inline).
450:     pub async fn run(
451:         &self,
452:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
453:         resume_state: Option<FlowRunState>,
454:     ) -> Result<FlowRunState, OrchestratorError> {
455:         // 1. Initialise or resume state
456:         let mut state = resume_state.unwrap_or_else(|| FlowRunState::new(&flow.name));
457: 
458:         // 2. Validate all task_file references at start (fail-fast)
459:         for step in &flow.steps {
460:             if let Some(ref task_file) = step.task_file {
461:                 let path = if std::path::Path::new(task_file).is_absolute() {
462:                     std::path::PathBuf::from(task_file)
463:                 } else {
464:                     self.working_dir.join(task_file)
465:                 };
466:                 if !path.exists() {
467:                     return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
468:                         flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
469:                         reason: format!("task_file not found: {}", path.display()),
470:                     });
471:                 }
472:             }
473:         }
474: 
475:         // 3. Calculate flow deadline for global timeout
476:         let flow_deadline = tokio::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(flow.timeout_secs);
477: 
478:         // 4. Execute steps sequentially from next_step_index
479:         let start_index = state.next_step_index;
480:         for (i, step) in flow.steps.iter().enumerate().skip(start_index) {
481:             state.next_step_index = i;
482: 
483:             // Check global flow timeout before each step
484:             if tokio::time::Instant::now() >= flow_deadline {
485:                 state.status = FlowRunStatus::Failed;
486:                 state.finished_at = Some(Utc::now());
487:                 state.error = Some(format!(
488:                     "flow '{}' exceeded global timeout of {}s",
489:                     flow.name, flow.timeout_secs
490:                 ));
491:                 let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
492:                 return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
493:                     flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
494:                     reason: format!("flow exceeded global timeout of {}s", flow.timeout_secs),
495:                 });
496:             }
497: 
498:             // Handle checkpoint step: persist state and pause
499:             if step.kind == StepKind::Checkpoint {
500:                 state.status = FlowRunStatus::Paused;
501:                 state.next_step_index = i + 1;
502:                 let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
503:                 tracing::info!(flow = %flow.name, step = %step.name, next_index = i + 1, "flow paused at checkpoint");
504:                 return Ok(state);
505:             }
506: 
507:             // Handle matrix expansion before dispatching to step executors.
508:             if step.matrix.is_some() {
509:                 match self.execute_matrix_step(step, flow, &state).await {
510:                     Ok(envelopes) => {
511:                         let result = MatrixResult::from_envelopes(&envelopes);
512:                         let had_failures = result.failure_count > 0;
513:                         state.matrix_envelopes.insert(step.name.clone(), envelopes);
514:                         state.next_step_index = i + 1;
515:                         let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
516:                         if had_failures {
517:                             tracing::warn!(
518:                                 step = %step.name,
519:                                 failures = result.failure_count,
520:                                 "matrix step completed with failures"
521:                             );
522:                         }
523:                     }
524:                     Err(e) => match step.on_fail {
525:                         FailStrategy::Abort => {
526:                             state.status = FlowRunStatus::Failed;
527:                             state.finished_at = Some(Utc::now());
528:                             state.error = Some(e.to_string());
529:                             let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
530:                             return Err(e);
531:                         }
532:                         FailStrategy::SkipFailed | FailStrategy::Continue => {
533:                             tracing::warn!(step = %step.name, error = %e, "matrix step failed, continuing");
534:                         }
535:                     },
536:                 }
537:                 continue;
538:             }
539: 
540:             let result = match step.kind {
541:                 StepKind::Action => self.execute_action(step, flow, &state).await,
542:                 StepKind::Agent => self.execute_agent(step, flow, &state).await,
543:                 StepKind::Gate => {
544:                     match self.evaluate_gate(step, flow, &state) {
545:                         Ok(true) => {
546:                             // Gate passed -- create a synthetic envelope
547:                             Ok(StepEnvelope {
548:                                 step_name: step.name.clone(),
549:                                 started_at: Utc::now(),
550:                                 finished_at: Utc::now(),
551:                                 exit_code: 0,
552:                                 stdout: "gate passed".to_string(),
553:                                 stderr: String::new(),
554:                                 cost_usd: None,
555:                                 session_id: None,
556:                                 input_tokens: None,
557:                                 output_tokens: None,
558:                                 stdout_file: None,
559:                             })
560:                         }
561:                         Ok(false) => {
562:                             // Gate failed
563:                             match step.on_fail {
564:                                 FailStrategy::Abort => {
565:                                     // Record the gate failure before aborting
566:                                     let gate_envelope = StepEnvelope {
567:                                         step_name: step.name.clone(),
568:                                         started_at: Utc::now(),
569:                                         finished_at: Utc::now(),
570:                                         exit_code: 1,
571:                                         stdout: "gate failed".to_string(),
572:                                         stderr: String::new(),
573:                                         cost_usd: None,
574:                                         session_id: None,
575:                                         input_tokens: None,
576:                                         output_tokens: None,
577:                                         stdout_file: None,
578:                                     };
579:                                     state.step_envelopes.push(gate_envelope);
580:                                     state.status = FlowRunStatus::Aborted;
581:                                     state.finished_at = Some(Utc::now());
582:                                     state.error = Some(format!("gate '{}' rejected", step.name));
583:                                     let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
584:                                     return Ok(state);
585:                                 }
586:                                 FailStrategy::SkipFailed | FailStrategy::Continue => {
587:                                     // Create a synthetic failed envelope and continue
588:                                     Ok(StepEnvelope {
589:                                         step_name: step.name.clone(),
590:                                         started_at: Utc::now(),
591:                                         finished_at: Utc::now(),
592:                                         exit_code: 1,
593:                                         stdout: "gate failed".to_string(),
594:                                         stderr: String::new(),
595:                                         cost_usd: None,
596:                                         session_id: None,
597:                                         input_tokens: None,
598:                                         output_tokens: None,
599:                                         stdout_file: None,
600:                                     })
601:                                 }
602:                             }
603:                         }
604:                         Err(e) => Err(e),
605:                     }
606:                 }
607:                 StepKind::Checkpoint => {
608:                     unreachable!("Checkpoint should be handled before the match")
609:                 }
610:             };
611: 
612:             match result {
613:                 Ok(mut envelope) => {
614:                     // Write stdout to temp file for downstream action steps
615:                     let stdout_file_path =
616:                         format!("/tmp/flow-{}-{}.stdout", state.correlation_id, step.name);
617:                     if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&stdout_file_path, &envelope.stdout) {
618:                         tracing::warn!(step = %step.name, error = %e, "failed to write stdout temp file");
619:                     } else {
620:                         envelope.stdout_file = Some(stdout_file_path);
621:                     }
622: 
623:                     state.step_envelopes.push(envelope);
624:                     state.next_step_index = i + 1;
625:                     let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
626:                 }
627:                 Err(e) => match step.on_fail {
628:                     FailStrategy::Abort => {
629:                         state.status = FlowRunStatus::Failed;
630:                         state.finished_at = Some(Utc::now());
631:                         state.error = Some(e.to_string());
632:                         let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
633:                         return Err(e);
634:                     }
635:                     FailStrategy::SkipFailed | FailStrategy::Continue => {
636:                         tracing::warn!(step = %step.name, error = %e, "step failed, continuing per on_fail policy");
637:                         continue;
638:                     }
639:                 },
640:             }
641:         }
642: 
643:         // 4. All steps complete
644:         state.status = FlowRunStatus::Completed;
645:         state.finished_at = Some(Utc::now());
646:         let _ = state.save_to_file(&self.flow_state_dir);
647: 
648:         // Temp file cleanup: stdout files in /tmp/flow-{correlation_id}-{step_name}.stdout
649:         // are intentionally NOT cleaned up here to preserve debug data.
650:         // Cleanup happens on orchestrator restart via a sweep of /tmp/flow-* files.
651: 
652:         Ok(state)
653:     }
654: 
655:     /// Resolve template variables in a string.
656:     /// Supports: {{repo_path}}, {{base_branch}}, {{flow.name}}, {{flow.correlation_id}},
657:     /// {{issue}}, `{{steps.<name>.stdout}}`, `{{steps.<name>.stderr}}`, `{{steps.<name>.exit_code}}`,
658:     /// `{{steps.<name>.stdout_file}}`
659:     pub fn resolve_templates(
660:         &self,
661:         template: &str,
662:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
663:         state: &FlowRunState,
664:     ) -> String {
665:         let mut result = template.to_string();
666:         result = result.replace("{{repo_path}}", &flow.repo_path);
667:         result = result.replace("{{base_branch}}", &flow.base_branch);
668:         result = result.replace("{{flow.name}}", &flow.name);
669:         result = result.replace("{{flow.correlation_id}}", &state.correlation_id.to_string());
670:         if let Some(ref issue) = state.issue {
671:             result = result.replace("{{issue}}", issue);
672:         }
673: 
674:         // Resolve step references: {{steps.<name>.stdout}}, etc.
675:         for envelope in &state.step_envelopes {
676:             let prefix = format!("{{{{steps.{}", envelope.step_name);
677:             if result.contains(&prefix) {
678:                 result = result.replace(
679:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.stdout}}}}", envelope.step_name),
680:                     &envelope.stdout,
681:                 );
682:                 result = result.replace(
683:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.stderr}}}}", envelope.step_name),
684:                     &envelope.stderr,
685:                 );
686:                 result = result.replace(
687:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.exit_code}}}}", envelope.step_name),
688:                     &envelope.exit_code.to_string(),
689:                 );
690:                 if let Some(ref stdout_file) = envelope.stdout_file {
691:                     result = result.replace(
692:                         &format!("{{{{steps.{}.stdout_file}}}}", envelope.step_name),
693:                         stdout_file,
694:                     );
695:                 }
696:             }
697:         }
698: 
699:         // Resolve matrix aggregate references: {{steps.<name>.success_count}}, {{steps.<name>.all_exit_codes}}
700:         for (step_name, envelopes) in &state.matrix_envelopes {
701:             let prefix = format!("{{{{steps.{}", step_name);
702:             if result.contains(&prefix) {
703:                 let agg = MatrixResult::from_envelopes(envelopes);
704:                 result = result.replace(
705:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.success_count}}}}", step_name),
706:                     &agg.success_count.to_string(),
707:                 );
708:                 result = result.replace(
709:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.failure_count}}}}", step_name),
710:                     &agg.failure_count.to_string(),
711:                 );
712:                 result = result.replace(
713:                     &format!("{{{{steps.{}.all_exit_codes}}}}", step_name),
714:                     &agg.all_exit_codes,
715:                 );
716:             }
717:         }
718: 
719:         // Remove any unresolved {{...}} references (resolve to empty string)
720:         let re = regex::Regex::new(r"\{\{[^}]+\}\}").unwrap();
721:         result = re.replace_all(&result, "").to_string();
722: 
723:         result
724:     }
725: 
726:     /// Execute a single sub-step of a matrix expansion. Resolves `{{matrix.*}}`
727:     /// variables from `matrix_row` before dispatching to the underlying executor.
728:     async fn execute_matrix_sub_step(
729:         &self,
730:         step: &FlowStepDef,
731:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
732:         state: &FlowRunState,
733:         matrix_row: &MatrixParams,
734:         sub_index: usize,
735:     ) -> Result<StepEnvelope, OrchestratorError> {
736:         let sub_name = format!("{}-matrix-{}", step.name, sub_index);
737:         let mut sub_step = step.clone();
738:         sub_step.name = sub_name;
739:         sub_step.matrix = None; // prevent recursion
740: 
741:         // Resolve {{matrix.*}} in mutable fields.
742:         // Priority: matrix_row has key -> use that value directly (overrides step default).
743:         // Otherwise resolve any {{matrix.*}} placeholders in the step's value.
744:         if let Some(ref task) = sub_step.task {
745:             sub_step.task = Some(resolve_matrix_vars(task, matrix_row));
746:         }
747:         if let Some(ref task_file) = sub_step.task_file {
748:             sub_step.task_file = Some(resolve_matrix_vars(task_file, matrix_row));
749:         }
750:         if let Some(ref command) = sub_step.command {
751:             sub_step.command = Some(resolve_matrix_vars(command, matrix_row));
752:         }
753:         if let Some(ref model) = sub_step.model {
754:             sub_step.model = Some(resolve_matrix_vars(model, matrix_row));
755:         }
756:         if let Some(ref provider) = sub_step.provider {
757:             sub_step.provider = Some(resolve_matrix_vars(provider, matrix_row));
758:         }
759:         // cli_tool: matrix_row overrides step default if present
760:         if let Some(val) = matrix_row.get("cli_tool") {
761:             sub_step.cli_tool = Some(val.clone());
762:         }
763:         // fallback_provider: matrix_row overrides step default if present
764:         if let Some(val) = matrix_row.get("fallback_provider") {
765:             sub_step.fallback_provider = Some(val.clone());
766:         }
767:         // fallback_model: matrix_row overrides step default if present
768:         if let Some(val) = matrix_row.get("fallback_model") {
769:             sub_step.fallback_model = Some(val.clone());
770:         }
771: 
772:         match sub_step.kind {
773:             StepKind::Action => self.execute_action(&sub_step, flow, state).await,
774:             StepKind::Agent => self.execute_agent(&sub_step, flow, state).await,
775:             _ => Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
776:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
777:                 reason: format!(
778:                     "matrix step '{}': only 'action' and 'agent' kinds support matrix expansion",
779:                     step.name
780:                 ),
781:             }),
782:         }
783:     }
784: 
785:     /// Execute a matrix-expanded step: fan out into N sub-executions (one per
786:     /// params row), run sequentially (respecting `max_parallel` as advisory),
787:     /// and collect all results.
788:     ///
789:     /// Returns the ordered envelopes. If `fail_strategy` is `Abort` and any
790:     /// sub-step fails, returns an error after all sub-steps are recorded.
791:     pub async fn execute_matrix_step(
792:         &self,
793:         step: &FlowStepDef,
794:         flow: &FlowDefinition,
795:         state: &FlowRunState,
796:     ) -> Result<Vec<StepEnvelope>, OrchestratorError> {
797:         let matrix = step
798:             .matrix
799:             .as_ref()
800:             .ok_or_else(|| OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
801:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
802:                 reason: format!(
803:                     "execute_matrix_step called on step '{}' with no matrix config",
804:                     step.name
805:                 ),
806:             })?;
807: 
808:         if matrix.params.is_empty() {
809:             return Ok(vec![]);
810:         }
811: 
812:         // Guard: cap params count to prevent unbounded sequential execution.
813:         if matrix.params.len() > MAX_MATRIX_PARAMS {
814:             return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
815:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
816:                 reason: format!(
817:                     "matrix step '{}': params count {} exceeds maximum {}",
818:                     step.name,
819:                     matrix.params.len(),
820:                     MAX_MATRIX_PARAMS
821:                 ),
822:             });
823:         }
824: 
825:         // Guard: validate all param values for shell metacharacters before any
826:         // sub-step is executed, so we fail fast rather than mid-run.
827:         for row in &matrix.params {
828:             for (key, value) in row {
829:                 validate_matrix_param(&flow.name, &step.name, key, value)?;
830:             }
831:         }
832: 
833:         let fail_strategy = matrix.fail_strategy;
834:         let mut envelopes = Vec::with_capacity(matrix.params.len());
835:         let mut any_failed = false;
836: 
837:         // Run sub-executions sequentially. The max_parallel field is preserved
838:         // for future concurrency support without a breaking API change.
839:         for (idx, row) in matrix.params.iter().enumerate() {
840:             match self
841:                 .execute_matrix_sub_step(step, flow, state, row, idx)
842:                 .await
843:             {
844:                 Ok(env) => {
845:                     if env.exit_code != 0 {
846:                         any_failed = true;
847:                     }
848:                     envelopes.push(env);
849:                 }
850:                 Err(e) => {
851:                     any_failed = true;
852:                     envelopes.push(StepEnvelope {
853:                         step_name: format!("{}-matrix-{}-error", step.name, idx),
854:                         started_at: Utc::now(),
855:                         finished_at: Utc::now(),
856:                         exit_code: -1,
857:                         stdout: String::new(),
858:                         stderr: e.to_string(),
859:                         cost_usd: None,
860:                         session_id: None,
861:                         input_tokens: None,
862:                         output_tokens: None,
863:                         stdout_file: None,
864:                     });
865:                 }
866:             }
867:         }
868: 
869:         if any_failed && fail_strategy == FailStrategy::Abort {
870:             return Err(OrchestratorError::FlowFailed {
871:                 flow_name: flow.name.clone(),
872:                 reason: format!(
873:                     "matrix step '{}': one or more sub-executions failed",
874:                     step.name
875:                 ),
876:             });
877:         }
878: 
879:         Ok(envelopes)
880:     }
881: }

1014:         let executor = FlowExecutor::new(PathBuf::from("/tmp"), PathBuf::from("/tmp/state"));
1015:         let flow = create_test_flow();
1016: 
1017:         let mut state = FlowRunState::new("test-flow");
1018:         state.step_envelopes.push(StepEnvelope {
1019:             step_name: "step1".to_string(),
1020:             started_at: Utc::now(),
1021:             finished_at: Utc::now(),
1022:             exit_code: 0,
1023:             stdout: "output1".to_string(),
1024:             stderr: "stderr1".to_string(),
1025:             cost_usd: None,
1026:             session_id: None,
1027:             input_tokens: None,
1028:             output_tokens: None,
1029:             stdout_file: None,
1030:         });
1031:         state.step_envelopes.push(StepEnvelope {
1032:             step_name: "step2".to_string(),
1033:             started_at: Utc::now(),
1034:             finished_at: Utc::now(),
1035:             exit_code: 1,
1036:             stdout: "output2".to_string(),
1037:             stderr: "stderr2".to_string(),
1038:             cost_usd: None,
1039:             session_id: None,
1040:             input_tokens: None,
1041:             output_tokens: None,
1042:             stdout_file: None,

1800:             repo_path: dir.to_string_lossy().to_string(),
1801:             base_branch: "main".to_string(),
1802:             timeout_secs: 3600,
1803:             steps: vec![
1804:                 FlowStepDef {
1805:                     name: "step1".to_string(),
1806:                     kind: StepKind::Action,
1807:                     command: Some("echo hello".to_string()),
1808:                     cli_tool: None,
1809:                     model: None,
1810:                     task: None,
1811:                     task_file: None,
1812:                     condition: None,
1813:                     timeout_secs: 10,
1814:                     on_fail: FailStrategy::Abort,
1815:                     provider: None,
1816:                     persona: None,
1817:                     fallback_provider: None,
1818:                     fallback_model: None,
1819: 
1820:                     matrix: None,
1821:                 },
1822:                 FlowStepDef {
1823:                     name: "checkpoint1".to_string(),
1824:                     kind: StepKind::Checkpoint,
1825:                     command: None,
1826:                     cli_tool: None,
1827:                     model: None,
1828:                     task: None,
1829:                     task_file: None,
1830:                     condition: None,
1831:                     timeout_secs: 10,
1832:                     on_fail: FailStrategy::Abort,
1833:                     provider: None,
1834:                     persona: None,
1835:                     fallback_provider: None,
1836:                     fallback_model: None,
1837: 
1838:                     matrix: None,
1839:                 },
1840:                 FlowStepDef {
1841:                     name: "step2".to_string(),
1842:                     kind: StepKind::Action,
1843:                     command: Some("echo world".to_string()),
1844:                     cli_tool: None,
1845:                     model: None,
1846:                     task: None,
1847:                     task_file: None,
1848:                     condition: None,
1849:                     timeout_secs: 10,
1850:                     on_fail: FailStrategy::Abort,
1851:                     provider: None,
1852:                     persona: None,
1853:                     fallback_provider: None,
1854:                     fallback_model: None,
1855: 
1856:                     matrix: None,
1857:                 },
1858:             ],
1859:         };
1860: 
1861:         let result = executor.run(&flow, None).await.unwrap();
1862: 
1863:         // Flow should be paused, not completed
1864:         assert_eq!(result.status, FlowRunStatus::Paused);
1865:         // Only step1 should have run
1866:         assert_eq!(result.step_envelopes.len(), 1);
1867:         assert_eq!(result.step_envelopes[0].step_name, "step1");
1868:         // Next step index should be past the checkpoint (index 2, which is step2)
1869:         assert_eq!(result.next_step_index, 2);
1870: 
1871:         // Resume from checkpoint -- should run step2 and complete
1872:         let resumed = executor.run(&flow, Some(result)).await.unwrap();
1873:         assert_eq!(resumed.status, FlowRunStatus::Completed);
1874:         // Should now have 2 step envelopes (step1 from before + step2 from resume)
1875:         assert_eq!(resumed.step_envelopes.len(), 2);
1876:         assert_eq!(resumed.step_envelopes[1].step_name, "step2");
1877: 
1878:         // Clean up test state dir
1879:         let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&state_dir);
1880:     }

2007:     async fn test_matrix_step_abort_on_failure() {
2008:         let temp_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
2009:         let executor =
2010:             FlowExecutor::new(temp_dir.path().to_path_buf(), temp_dir.path().to_path_buf());
2011: 
2012:         use std::collections::HashMap;
2013: 
2014:         let mut row0 = HashMap::new();
2015:         row0.insert("code".to_string(), "0".to_string());
2016:         let mut row1 = HashMap::new();
2017:         row1.insert("code".to_string(), "1".to_string());
2018: 
2019:         let flow = FlowDefinition {
2020:             name: "matrix-abort-test".to_string(),
2021:             project: "test".to_string(),
2022:             schedule: None,
2023:             repo_path: "/tmp/repo".to_string(),
2024:             base_branch: "main".to_string(),
2025:             timeout_secs: 3600,
2026:             steps: vec![FlowStepDef {
2027:                 name: "exit-matrix".to_string(),
2028:                 kind: StepKind::Action,
2029:                 command: Some("exit {{matrix.code}}".to_string()),
2030:                 cli_tool: None,
2031:                 model: None,
2032:                 task: None,
2033:                 task_file: None,
2034:                 condition: None,
2035:                 timeout_secs: 10,
2036:                 on_fail: FailStrategy::Abort,
2037:                 provider: None,
2038:                 persona: None,
2039:                 fallback_provider: None,
2040:                 fallback_model: None,
2041: 
2042:                 matrix: Some(MatrixConfig {
2043:                     params: vec![row0, row1],
2044:                     max_parallel: 1,
2045:                     fail_strategy: FailStrategy::Abort,
2046:                 }),
2047:             }],
2048:         };
2049: 
2050:         // When matrix.fail_strategy=abort and a sub-step fails, execute_matrix_step returns Err.
2051:         // The run loop honours the step-level on_fail=Abort and marks the flow Failed.
2052:         let result = executor.run(&flow, None).await;
2053:         // Flow should fail because matrix step aborted on non-zero exit code
2054:         assert!(result.is_err() || result.as_ref().unwrap().status == FlowRunStatus::Failed);
2055:     }

2100:     #[test]
2101:     fn test_validate_matrix_param_rejects_shell_metacharacters() {
2102:         // Semicolons enable command chaining.
2103:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "hello; rm -rf /").is_err());
2104:         // Backtick subshell.
2105:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "`id`").is_err());
2106:         // Dollar-paren subshell.
2107:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "$(id)").is_err());
2108:         // Pipe.
2109:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "foo | bar").is_err());
2110:         // Ampersand background.
2111:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "cmd &").is_err());
2112:         // Newline injection.
2113:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "first\nsecond").is_err());
2114:         // Redirect.
2115:         assert!(validate_matrix_param("flow", "step", "k", "a > /etc/passwd").is_err());
2116:     }
